raw_consumer_statement:
I have a Citi Double Cash credit card for the last 6 years or so. I used it purchase a seasonal lawn mowing service on XX/XX/XXXX and was charged USD XXXX. 

The merchant was supposed to come the next day to start, but he didnt and went incommunicado, after multiple attempts to reach him over email and call, he responded that he is in some trouble with IRS and has to shut shop and that he will refund our money as soon as he can. We continued to follow up over email about the refund, but fell on deaf ears with no response since. 

Meanwhile raised a dispute on the Citi app on XX/XX/XXXX. Never received any status updates from Citi in the app or mail, but suddenly XXXX day in early XXXX saw the dispute status changed to customer responsible which was updated on XX/XX/XXXX. It said that they contacted the merchant and he said the services were delivered!. I called Citi bank customer care multiple times, most times there was a long hold time and the one time in late XXXX I was able to talk to a human they put me through different people and finally their agent said we sent you a notice on email to submit documents. Strangely, that doesnt show up in the app nor was it posted on snail mail but the agent asked me to send documentation over email. I did that right after, and it took them over 3 months to respond with their outcome which is not favorable as they only credited XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX without any explanation for partial credit. 


The services were never delivered to me and there is no reason I shouldnt get the full credit when there is a confirmation from the merchant that they have shut shop and cant mow our lawn!
